'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, specializing in {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: - {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群}} - {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群}} - {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}, specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==

{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館}} | Operated_by = {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}} | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}} 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, specializing in {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: - {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群}} - {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群}} - {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}, specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==
{{reflist|2|refs= <ref name=hyogo>{{ill|Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education|ja|兵庫県教育委員会}}, "National Ranking of Archaeological Sites and Relic Quantities" ([http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/kihonkousou/6%284%29.pdf PDF])</ref> <ref name=zen>『Compilation of Keyhole-Shaped Kofun: Tohoku and Kanto Edition』 ISBN 978-4-634-50010-5</ref> <ref name=concept>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/info/prof.html Museum Purpose and Management Goals]</ref> <ref name=chibatopi>Chiba Nippo: "[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/local_kiji.php?i=nesp1290132141 1000-Year-Old Everyday Life Revealed Through Ink-Inscribed Pottery]" (November 19, 2010)</ref> <ref name=haniwa1>Nihon University Press: "[http://www.nu-press.net/archives/article000811.html Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Hosts Haniwa Festival, Displaying Around 20 Warrior Haniwa]" (July 23, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa2>Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Museum: "[http://www.chs.nihon-u.ac.jp/museum/exhibition.php?l=3&r=110131-163501 Haniwa Festival – Expressions in Clay]" (June 18 – July 28, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa3>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html Haniwa Festival]</ref> <ref name=haniwa4>Narita City Official Site: "[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/kanko/guide/sight-old-haniwa.html Narita City Tourism Guide]"</ref> <ref name=welcome>Ministry of Foreign Affairs: "[https://www.mlit.go.jp/kisha/kisha02/01/010704/010704_3_04.pdf Cultural and Tourism Promotion by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs]" (April 25, 2002)</ref> }}== See Also ==

{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館}} | Operated_by = {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}} | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}} 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, specializing in {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: - {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群}} - {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群}} - {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}, specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==
{{reflist|2|refs= <ref name=hyogo>{{ill|Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education|ja|兵庫県教育委員会}}, "National Ranking of Archaeological Sites and Relic Quantities" ([http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/kihonkousou/6%284%29.pdf PDF])</ref> <ref name=zen>『Compilation of Keyhole-Shaped Kofun: Tohoku and Kanto Edition』 ISBN 978-4-634-50010-5</ref> <ref name=concept>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/info/prof.html Museum Purpose and Management Goals]</ref> <ref name=chibatopi>Chiba Nippo: "[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/local_kiji.php?i=nesp1290132141 1000-Year-Old Everyday Life Revealed Through Ink-Inscribed Pottery]" (November 19, 2010)</ref> <ref name=haniwa1>Nihon University Press: "[http://www.nu-press.net/archives/article000811.html Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Hosts Haniwa Festival, Displaying Around 20 Warrior Haniwa]" (July 23, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa2>Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Museum: "[http://www.chs.nihon-u.ac.jp/museum/exhibition.php?l=3&r=110131-163501 Haniwa Festival – Expressions in Clay]" (June 18 – July 28, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa3>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html Haniwa Festival]</ref> <ref name=haniwa4>Narita City Official Site: "[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/kanko/guide/sight-old-haniwa.html Narita City Tourism Guide]"</ref> <ref name=welcome>Ministry of Foreign Affairs: "[https://www.mlit.go.jp/kisha/kisha02/01/010704/010704_3_04.pdf Cultural and Tourism Promotion by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs]" (April 25, 2002)</ref> }}== See Also ==

{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館}} | Operated_by = {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}} | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}} 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, specializing in {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: - {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群}} - {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群}} - {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}, specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==

{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館}} | Operated_by = {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}} | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}} 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, specializing in {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: - {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群}} - {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群}} - {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}, specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==
{{reflist|2|refs= <ref name=hyogo>{{ill|Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education|ja|兵庫県教育委員会}}, "National Ranking of Archaeological Sites and Relic Quantities" ([http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/kihonkousou/6%284%29.pdf PDF])</ref> <ref name=zen>『Compilation of Keyhole-Shaped Kofun: Tohoku and Kanto Edition』 ISBN 978-4-634-50010-5</ref> <ref name=concept>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/info/prof.html Museum Purpose and Management Goals]</ref> <ref name=chibatopi>Chiba Nippo: "[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/local_kiji.php?i=nesp1290132141 1000-Year-Old Everyday Life Revealed Through Ink-Inscribed Pottery]" (November 19, 2010)</ref> <ref name=haniwa1>Nihon University Press: "[http://www.nu-press.net/archives/article000811.html Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Hosts Haniwa Festival, Displaying Around 20 Warrior Haniwa]" (July 23, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa2>Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Museum: "[http://www.chs.nihon-u.ac.jp/museum/exhibition.php?l=3&r=110131-163501 Haniwa Festival – Expressions in Clay]" (June 18 – July 28, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa3>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html Haniwa Festival]</ref> <ref name=haniwa4>Narita City Official Site: "[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/kanko/guide/sight-old-haniwa.html Narita City Tourism Guide]"</ref> <ref name=welcome>Ministry of Foreign Affairs: "[https://www.mlit.go.jp/kisha/kisha02/01/010704/010704_3_04.pdf Cultural and Tourism Promotion by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs]" (April 25, 2002)</ref> }} == See Also ==
* {{ill|Haniwa Day|ja|埴輪の日}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of historical sites in the Kanto region|ja|関東地方の史跡一覧}} * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群}} * {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}} * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} * {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} * {{ill|Bōsō Umayama Shell Mound|ja|房総姥山貝塚}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.haniwakan.com/index.html Official Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Website] * [http://www.chiba-web.com/chibahaku/24/index.html Chiba Prefecture Museum Association - Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum]

'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, specializing in {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}, specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>

{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館}} | Operated_by = {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}} | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}} 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, specializing in {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}, specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>

{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館}} | Operated_by = {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}} | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}} 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, specializing in {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}, specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==
{{reflist|2|refs= <ref name=hyogo>{{ill|Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education|ja|兵庫県教育委員会}}, "National Ranking of Archaeological Sites and Relic Quantities" ([http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/kihonkousou/6%284%29.pdf PDF])</ref> <ref name=zen>『Compilation of Keyhole-Shaped Kofun: Tohoku and Kanto Edition』 ISBN 978-4-634-50010-5</ref> <ref name=concept>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/info/prof.html Museum Purpose and Management Goals]</ref> <ref name=chibatopi>Chiba Nippo: "[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/local_kiji.php?i=nesp1290132141 1000-Year-Old Everyday Life Revealed Through Ink-Inscribed Pottery]" (November 19, 2010)</ref> <ref name=haniwa1>Nihon University Press: "[http://www.nu-press.net/archives/article000811.html Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Hosts Haniwa Festival, Displaying Around 20 Warrior Haniwa]" (July 23, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa2>Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Museum: "[http://www.chs.nihon-u.ac.jp/museum/exhibition.php?l=3&r=110131-163501 Haniwa Festival – Expressions in Clay]" (June 18 – July 28, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa3>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html Haniwa Festival]</ref> <ref name=haniwa4>Narita City Official Site: "[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/kanko/guide/sight-old-haniwa.html Narita City Tourism Guide]"</ref> <ref name=welcome>Ministry of Foreign Affairs: "[https://www.mlit.go.jp/kisha/kisha02/01/010704/010704_3_04.pdf Cultural and Tourism Promotion by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs]" (April 25, 2002)</ref> }} == See Also ==
* {{ill|Haniwa Day|ja|埴輪の日}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of historical sites in the Kanto region|ja|関東地方の史跡一覧}} * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群}} * {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}} * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} * {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|駄ノ塚古墳}} * {{ill|Bōsō Umayama Shell Mound|ja|房総姥山貝塚}}

'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, specializing in {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}, specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==

{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館}} | Operated_by = {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}} | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}} 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, specializing in {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}, specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==
{{reflist|2|refs= <ref name=hyogo>{{ill|Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education|ja|兵庫県教育委員会}}, "National Ranking of Archaeological Sites and Relic Quantities" ([http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/kihonkousou/6%284%29.pdf PDF])</ref> <ref name=zen>『Compilation of Keyhole-Shaped Kofun: Tohoku and Kanto Edition』 ISBN 978-4-634-50010-5</ref> <ref name=concept>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/info/prof.html Museum Purpose and Management Goals]</ref> <ref name=chibatopi>Chiba Nippo: "[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/local_kiji.php?i=nesp1290132141 1000-Year-Old Everyday Life Revealed Through Ink-Inscribed Pottery]" (November 19, 2010)</ref> <ref name=haniwa1>Nihon University Press: "[http://www.nu-press.net/archives/article000811.html Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Hosts Haniwa Festival, Displaying Around 20 Warrior Haniwa]" (July 23, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa2>Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Museum: "[http://www.chs.nihon-u.ac.jp/museum/exhibition.php?l=3&r=110131-163501 Haniwa Festival – Expressions in Clay]" (June 18 – July 28, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa3>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html Haniwa Festival]</ref> <ref name=haniwa4>Narita City Official Site: "[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/kanko/guide/sight-old-haniwa.html Narita City Tourism Guide]"</ref> <ref name=welcome>Ministry of Foreign Affairs: "[https://www.mlit.go.jp/kisha/kisha02/01/010704/010704_3_04.pdf Cultural and Tourism Promotion by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs]" (April 25, 2002)</ref> }} == See Also ==
* {{ill|Haniwa Day|ja|埴輪の日}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of historical sites in the Kanto region|ja|関東地方の史跡一覧}} * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群}} * {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}} * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} * {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Bōsō Umayama Shell Mound|ja|房総姥山貝塚}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.haniwakan.com/index.html Official Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Website] * [http://www.chiba-web.com/chibahaku/24/index.html Chiba Prefecture Museum Association - Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum]

Chiba Prefecture ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>

=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>

* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>

revision.text:
{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館}} | Operated_by = {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}} | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}} 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, specializing in {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}, specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==
{{reflist|2|refs= <ref name=hyogo>{{ill|Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education|ja|兵庫県教育委員会}}, "National Ranking of Archaeological Sites and Relic Quantities" ([http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/kihonkousou/6%284%29.pdf PDF])</ref> <ref name=zen>『Compilation of Keyhole-Shaped Kofun: Tohoku and Kanto Edition』 ISBN 978-4-634-50010-5</ref> <ref name=concept>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/info/prof.html Museum Purpose and Management Goals]</ref> <ref name=chibatopi>Chiba Nippo: "[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/local_kiji.php?i=nesp1290132141 1000-Year-Old Everyday Life Revealed Through Ink-Inscribed Pottery]" (November 19, 2010)</ref> <ref name=haniwa1>Nihon University Press: "[http://www.nu-press.net/archives/article000811.html Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Hosts Haniwa Festival, Displaying Around 20 Warrior Haniwa]" (July 23, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa2>Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Museum: "[http://www.chs.nihon-u.ac.jp/museum/exhibition.php?l=3&r=110131-163501 Haniwa Festival – Expressions in Clay]" (June 18 – July 28, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa3>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html Haniwa Festival]</ref> <ref name=haniwa4>Narita City Official Site: "[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/kanko/guide/sight-old-haniwa.html Narita City Tourism Guide]"</ref> <ref name=welcome>Ministry of Foreign Affairs: "[https://www.mlit.go.jp/kisha/kisha02/01/010704/010704_3_04.pdf Cultural and Tourism Promotion by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs]" (April 25, 2002)</ref> }} == See Also ==
* {{ill|Haniwa Day|ja|埴輪の日}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of historical sites in the Kanto region|ja|関東地方の史跡一覧}} * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群}} * {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}} * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳}} * {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Bōsō Umayama Shell Mound|ja|房総姥山貝塚}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.haniwakan.com/index.html Official Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Website] * [http://www.chiba-web.com/chibahaku/24/index.html Chiba Prefecture Museum Association - Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum]

{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館|12=draft}} | Operated_by = {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町|12=draft}} | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会|12=draft}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町|12=draft}} 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}, specializing in {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofuns) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|12=draft}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|12=draft}}, specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|12=draft}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>

{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館|12=draft}} | Operated_by = {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町|12=draft}} | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会|12=draft}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町|12=draft}} 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}, specializing in {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofuns) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|12=draft}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|12=draft}}, specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|12=draft}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==
{{reflist|2|refs= <ref name=hyogo>{{ill|Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education|ja|兵庫県教育委員会|12=draft}}, "National Ranking of Archaeological Sites and Relic Quantities" ([http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/kihonkousou/6%284%29.pdf PDF])</ref> <ref name=zen>『Compilation of Keyhole-Shaped Kofun: Tohoku and Kanto Edition』 ISBN 978-4-634-50010-5</ref> <ref name=concept>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/info/prof.html Museum Purpose and Management Goals]</ref> <ref name=chibatopi>Chiba Nippo: "[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/local_kiji.php?i=nesp1290132141 1000-Year-Old Everyday Life Revealed Through Ink-Inscribed Pottery]" (November 19, 2010)</ref> <ref name=haniwa1>Nihon University Press: "[http://www.nu-press.net/archives/article000811.html Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Hosts Haniwa Festival, Displaying Around 20 Warrior Haniwa]" (July 23, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa2>Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Museum: "[http://www.chs.nihon-u.ac.jp/museum/exhibition.php?l=3&r=110131-163501 Haniwa Festival – Expressions in Clay]" (June 18 – July 28, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa3>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html Haniwa Festival]</ref> <ref name=haniwa4>Narita City Official Site: "[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/kanko/guide/sight-old-haniwa.html Narita City Tourism Guide]"</ref> <ref name=welcome>Ministry of Foreign Affairs: "[https://www.mlit.go.jp/kisha/kisha02/01/010704/010704_3_04.pdf Cultural and Tourism Promotion by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs]" (April 25, 2002)</ref> }} == See Also ==
* {{ill|Haniwa Day|ja|埴輪の日|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of historical sites in the Kanto region|ja|関東地方の史跡一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Bōsō Umayama Shell Mound|ja|房総姥山貝塚|12=draft}}

=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|12=draft}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>

{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館|12=draft}} | Operated_by = {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町|12=draft}} | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会|12=draft}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町|12=draft}} 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}, specializing in {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofuns) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|12=draft}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|12=draft}}, specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|12=draft}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==
{{reflist|2|refs= <ref name=hyogo>{{ill|Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education|ja|兵庫県教育委員会|12=draft}}, "National Ranking of Archaeological Sites and Relic Quantities" ([http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/kihonkousou/6%284%29.pdf PDF])</ref> <ref name=zen>『Compilation of Keyhole-Shaped Kofun: Tohoku and Kanto Edition』 ISBN 978-4-634-50010-5</ref> <ref name=concept>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/info/prof.html Museum Purpose and Management Goals]</ref> <ref name=chibatopi>Chiba Nippo: "[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/local_kiji.php?i=nesp1290132141 1000-Year-Old Everyday Life Revealed Through Ink-Inscribed Pottery]" (November 19, 2010)</ref> <ref name=haniwa1>Nihon University Press: "[http://www.nu-press.net/archives/article000811.html Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Hosts Haniwa Festival, Displaying Around 20 Warrior Haniwa]" (July 23, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa2>Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Museum: "[http://www.chs.nihon-u.ac.jp/museum/exhibition.php?l=3&r=110131-163501 Haniwa Festival – Expressions in Clay]" (June 18 – July 28, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa3>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html Haniwa Festival]</ref> <ref name=haniwa4>Narita City Official Site: "[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/kanko/guide/sight-old-haniwa.html Narita City Tourism Guide]"</ref> <ref name=welcome>Ministry of Foreign Affairs: "[https://www.mlit.go.jp/kisha/kisha02/01/010704/010704_3_04.pdf Cultural and Tourism Promotion by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs]" (April 25, 2002)</ref> }} == See Also ==
* {{ill|Haniwa Day|ja|埴輪の日|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of historical sites in the Kanto region|ja|関東地方の史跡一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Bōsō Umayama Shell Mound|ja|房総姥山貝塚|12=draft}}

To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|12=draft|12=draft}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==
{{reflist|2|refs= <ref name=hyogo>{{ill|Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education|ja|兵庫県教育委員会|12=draft|12=draft}}, "National Ranking of Archaeological Sites and Relic Quantities" ([http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/kihonkousou/6%284%29.pdf PDF])</ref> <ref name=zen>『Compilation of Keyhole-Shaped Kofun: Tohoku and Kanto Edition』 ISBN 978-4-634-50010-5</ref> <ref name=concept>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/info/prof.html Museum Purpose and Management Goals]</ref> <ref name=chibatopi>Chiba Nippo: "[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/local_kiji.php?i=nesp1290132141 1000-Year-Old Everyday Life Revealed Through Ink-Inscribed Pottery]" (November 19, 2010)</ref> <ref name=haniwa1>Nihon University Press: "[http://www.nu-press.net/archives/article000811.html Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Hosts Haniwa Festival, Displaying Around 20 Warrior Haniwa]" (July 23, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa2>Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Museum: "[http://www.chs.nihon-u.ac.jp/museum/exhibition.php?l=3&r=110131-163501 Haniwa Festival – Expressions in Clay]" (June 18 – July 28, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa3>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html Haniwa Festival]</ref> <ref name=haniwa4>Narita City Official Site: "[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/kanko/guide/sight-old-haniwa.html Narita City Tourism Guide]"</ref> <ref name=welcome>Ministry of Foreign Affairs: "[https://www.mlit.go.jp/kisha/kisha02/01/010704/010704_3_04.pdf Cultural and Tourism Promotion by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs]" (April 25, 2002)</ref> }} == See Also ==

{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} | Operated_by = {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町|12=draft|12=draft}} | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会|12=draft|12=draft}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町|12=draft|12=draft}} 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft|12=draft}}, specializing in {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (ancient Kofuns) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|1=Yamada-Takaraba Kofun Group|12=draft|12=draft}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|12=draft|12=draft}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==
{{reflist|2|refs= <ref name=hyogo>{{ill|Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education|ja|兵庫県教育委員会|12=draft|12=draft}}, "National Ranking of Archaeological Sites and Relic Quantities" ([http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/kihonkousou/6%284%29.pdf PDF])</ref> <ref name=zen>『Compilation of Keyhole-Shaped Kofun: Tohoku and Kanto Edition』 ISBN 978-4-634-50010-5</ref> <ref name=concept>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/info/prof.html Museum Purpose and Management Goals]</ref> <ref name=chibatopi>Chiba Nippo: "[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/local_kiji.php?i=nesp1290132141 1000-Year-Old Everyday Life Revealed Through Ink-Inscribed Pottery]" (November 19, 2010)</ref> <ref name=haniwa1>Nihon University Press: "[http://www.nu-press.net/archives/article000811.html Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Hosts Haniwa Festival, Displaying Around 20 Warrior Haniwa]" (July 23, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa2>Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Museum: "[http://www.chs.nihon-u.ac.jp/museum/exhibition.php?l=3&r=110131-163501 Haniwa Festival – Expressions in Clay]" (June 18 – July 28, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa3>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html Haniwa Festival]</ref> <ref name=haniwa4>Narita City Official Site: "[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/kanko/guide/sight-old-haniwa.html Narita City Tourism Guide]"</ref> <ref name=welcome>Ministry of Foreign Affairs: "[https://www.mlit.go.jp/kisha/kisha02/01/010704/010704_3_04.pdf Cultural and Tourism Promotion by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs]" (April 25, 2002)</ref> }} == See Also ==
