{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} | Operated_by = {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町|12=draft|12=draft}} | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会|12=draft|12=draft}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町|12=draft|12=draft}} 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft|12=draft}}, specializing in {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (ancient Kofuns) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture Ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-Ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|1=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|12=draft|12=draft}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==

revision.comment: 22 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:芝山古墳・はにわ博物館 up to rev 100639483
revision.text:
{{博物館 |名称=芝山古墳・はにわ博物館 |画像 = Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG |imagesize = 300 |画像説明=博物館建物 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} |正式名称=芝山町立芝山古墳・はにわ博物館<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html 芝山町立芝山古墳・はにわ博物館設置及び管理に関する条例]</ref> |愛称=haniwakan |前身= |専門分野=考古資料 |事業主体=芝山町 |管理運営=芝山町教育委員会 |開館= |所在地郵便番号=289-1619 |所在地=千葉県山武郡芝山町芝山438-1 |緯度度= 35 |緯度分 = 41 |緯度秒 = 39 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) =N |経度度=140 |経度分 = 25 |経度秒 = 42 |E(東経)及びW(西経) =E |地図国コード= JP |位置= }} '''芝山古墳・はにわ博物館'''（しばやまこふん・はにわはくぶつかん）は、[[千葉県]][[山武郡]][[芝山町]]にある芝山町立の[[古墳]]と[[埴輪]]の[[博物館]]である。
== 概要 == 千葉県は、古墳所在基数が全国第2位の13,112基、[[遺跡]]出土[[遺物]]量も全国3位に上り<ref name=hyogo/>、特に[[前方後円墳]]については677基が確認されており全都道府県中最多であり（2位は茨城県の445基）<ref name=zen/>、そして芝山町の周辺には、[[5世紀]]から[[8世紀]]頃にかけて造営されたと推定される古墳群が集中しており、埴輪等の出土品も豊富である。これらの歴史遺産を、小学生にも親しめるように「わかりやすく、かつ楽しく、現代からみた古墳時代を」とのコンセプト<ref name=concept/>に基づき展示する施設として、[[1988年]]（[[昭和]]63年）[[5月1日]]に、芝山公園内に開設された。
== 展示内容 == === 第1展示室 === * 古墳や埴輪に関する基礎的な理解を得るための展示。 * [[小川台古墳群]]、[[蕪木古墳群]]、[[山田・宝馬古墳群]]等、周辺の古墳から出土した埴輪群。 * [[芝山古墳群]]の殿塚古墳・姫塚古墳から出土した葬送埴輪列の再現展示。 === 第2展示室 === * 埴輪による復元衣装。 * 古墳時代の復元住居。 === 第3展示室 === * 古墳分布図。 * 仏像など古墳時代から仏教受容への移行を示す展示。 === その他 === * 芝山町周辺の遺跡出土物を中心とした企画展<ref name=chibatopi/>を開催している。
== 他研究機関との連携 == * [[日本大学]][[文理学部]]と協力して、展示や講演等の企画を行っている<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>。
== 観光資源としての役割 == * 芝山町のイベントとして毎年11月に開催され、古墳時代を再現する祭りとなっている『[[芝山はにわ祭]]』を支援している<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/>。 * [[外務省]]が推進する『成田国際観光モデル地区』の「成田ウェルカムカード」参加施設<ref name=welcome/>。
== 脚注 == {{reflist|2|refs= <ref name=hyogo>兵庫県教育委員会 兵庫県の遺跡・遺物数の全国的な位置（[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/kihonkousou/6%284%29.pdf pdfファイル]）</ref> <ref name=zen>『前方後円墳集成 東北・関東編』 ISBN 978-4-634-50010-5</ref> <ref name=concept>公式サイト：[http://www.haniwakan.com/info/prof.html 設立目的と運営目標]</ref> <ref name=chibatopi>千葉日報『[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/local_kiji.php?i=nesp1290132141 墨書土器が伝える1000年前の庶民生活]』- 2010年11月19日</ref> <ref name=haniwa1>日本大学新聞 学術ニュース『[http://www.nu-press.net/archives/article000811.html 文理 はにわまつり開催 武人埴輪など約20点展示]』- 2008年7月23日</ref> <ref name=haniwa2>日本大学文理学部資料館『[http://www.chs.nihon-u.ac.jp/museum/exhibition.php?l=3&r=110131-163501 はにわまつり〜造形に込められた想い〜]』- 2008年6月18日 - 7月28日</ref> <ref name=haniwa3>公式サイト：[http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html はにわ祭]</ref> <ref name=haniwa4>成田市公式サイト：[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/kanko/guide/sight-old-haniwa.html 成田市観光ガイド]</ref> <ref name=welcome>外務省：外務省及び在外公館による日本の文化・観光紹介（[https://www.mlit.go.jp/kisha/kisha02/01/010704/010704_3_04.pdf pdfファイル]） - 2002年4月25日</ref> }}
== 関連項目 == *[[埴輪の日]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[関東地方の史跡一覧]] * [[芝山古墳群]] * [[小川台古墳群]] * [[蕪木古墳群]] * [[山室姫塚古墳]] * [[大堤権現塚古墳]] * [[駄ノ塚古墳]] * [[山武姥山貝塚]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/index.html 芝山古墳・はにわ博物館ホームページ] * [http://www.chiba-web.com/chibahaku/24/index.html 千葉県博物館協会 芝山町立芝山古墳・はにわ博物館]

{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Operated_by = {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specializing in {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (ancient Kofuns) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture Ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-Ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|1=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==
{{reflist|2|refs= <ref name=hyogo>{{ill|Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education|ja|兵庫県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "National Ranking of Archaeological Sites and Relic Quantities" ([http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/kihonkousou/6%284%29.pdf PDF])</ref> <ref name=zen>『Compilation of Keyhole-Shaped Kofun: Tohoku and Kanto Edition』 ISBN 978-4-634-50010-5</ref> <ref name=concept>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/info/prof.html Museum Purpose and Management Goals]</ref> <ref name=chibatopi>Chiba Nippo: "[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/local_kiji.php?i=nesp1290132141 1000-Year-Old Everyday Life Revealed Through Ink-Inscribed Pottery]" (November 19, 2010)</ref> <ref name=haniwa1>Nihon University Press: "[http://www.nu-press.net/archives/article000811.html Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Hosts Haniwa Festival, Displaying Around 20 Warrior Haniwa]" (July 23, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa2>Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Museum: "[http://www.chs.nihon-u.ac.jp/museum/exhibition.php?l=3&r=110131-163501 Haniwa Festival – Expressions in Clay]" (June 18 – July 28, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa3>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html Haniwa Festival]</ref> <ref name=haniwa4>Narita City Official Site: "[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/kanko/guide/sight-old-haniwa.html Narita City Tourism Guide]"</ref> <ref name=welcome>Ministry of Foreign Affairs: "[https://www.mlit.go.jp/kisha/kisha02/01/010704/010704_3_04.pdf Cultural and Tourism Promotion by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs]" (April 25, 2002)</ref> }} == See Also ==

{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Operated_by = {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|ja|芝山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specializing in {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (ancient Kofuns) and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture Ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-Ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|1=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==
{{reflist|2|refs= <ref name=hyogo>{{ill|Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education|ja|兵庫県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "National Ranking of Archaeological Sites and Relic Quantities" ([http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/kihonkousou/6%284%29.pdf PDF])</ref> <ref name=zen>『Compilation of Keyhole-Shaped Kofun: Tohoku and Kanto Edition』 ISBN 978-4-634-50010-5</ref> <ref name=concept>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/info/prof.html Museum Purpose and Management Goals]</ref> <ref name=chibatopi>Chiba Nippo: "[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/local_kiji.php?i=nesp1290132141 1000-Year-Old Everyday Life Revealed Through Ink-Inscribed Pottery]" (November 19, 2010)</ref> <ref name=haniwa1>Nihon University Press: "[http://www.nu-press.net/archives/article000811.html Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Hosts Haniwa Festival, Displaying Around 20 Warrior Haniwa]" (July 23, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa2>Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Museum: "[http://www.chs.nihon-u.ac.jp/museum/exhibition.php?l=3&r=110131-163501 Haniwa Festival – Expressions in Clay]" (June 18 – July 28, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa3>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html Haniwa Festival]</ref> <ref name=haniwa4>Narita City Official Site: "[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/kanko/guide/sight-old-haniwa.html Narita City Tourism Guide]"</ref> <ref name=welcome>Ministry of Foreign Affairs: "[https://www.mlit.go.jp/kisha/kisha02/01/010704/010704_3_04.pdf Cultural and Tourism Promotion by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs]" (April 25, 2002)</ref> }} == See Also ==

{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archaeological museum|qq=}} | Operated_by = [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]] | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibayama Town Board of Education|qq=}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]] 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], specializing in [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) and [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture Ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-Ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawadai Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaburaki Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|1=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]], specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|qq=}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>

revision.text:
{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archaeological museum|qq=}} | Operated_by = [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]] | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibayama Town Board of Education|qq=}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]] 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], specializing in [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) and [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture Ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-Ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawadai Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaburaki Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|1=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]], specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|qq=}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==
{{reflist|2|refs= <ref name=hyogo>{{ill|Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education|ja|兵庫県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education|qq=}}, "National Ranking of Archaeological Sites and Relic Quantities" ([http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/kihonkousou/6%284%29.pdf PDF])</ref> <ref name=zen>『Compilation of Keyhole-Shaped Kofun: Tohoku and Kanto Edition』 ISBN 978-4-634-50010-5</ref> <ref name=concept>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/info/prof.html Museum Purpose and Management Goals]</ref> <ref name=chibatopi>Chiba Nippo: "[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/local_kiji.php?i=nesp1290132141 1000-Year-Old Everyday Life Revealed Through Ink-Inscribed Pottery]" (November 19, 2010)</ref> <ref name=haniwa1>Nihon University Press: "[http://www.nu-press.net/archives/article000811.html Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Hosts Haniwa Festival, Displaying Around 20 Warrior Haniwa]" (July 23, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa2>Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Museum: "[http://www.chs.nihon-u.ac.jp/museum/exhibition.php?l=3&r=110131-163501 Haniwa Festival – Expressions in Clay]" (June 18 – July 28, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa3>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html Haniwa Festival]</ref> <ref name=haniwa4>Narita City Official Site: "[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/kanko/guide/sight-old-haniwa.html Narita City Tourism Guide]"</ref> <ref name=welcome>Ministry of Foreign Affairs: "[https://www.mlit.go.jp/kisha/kisha02/01/010704/010704_3_04.pdf Cultural and Tourism Promotion by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs]" (April 25, 2002)</ref> }} == See Also ==
* {{ill|Haniwa Day|ja|埴輪の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa Day|qq=}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of historical sites in the Kanto region|ja|関東地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of historical sites in the Kanto region|qq=}} * [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawadai Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaburaki Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsutsumi Gongen-zuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Bōsō Umayama Shell Mound|ja|房総姥山貝塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Umayama Shell Mound|qq=}}
== External Links ==

revision.text:
{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archaeological museum|qq=}} | Operated_by = [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]] | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibayama Town Board of Education|qq=}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]] 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], specializing in [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) and [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture Ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-Ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawadai Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaburaki Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|1=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]], specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|qq=}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==
{{reflist|2|refs= <ref name=hyogo>{{ill|Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education|ja|兵庫県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education|qq=}}, "National Ranking of Archaeological Sites and Relic Quantities" ([http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/kihonkousou/6%284%29.pdf PDF])</ref> <ref name=zen>『Compilation of Keyhole-Shaped Kofun: Tohoku and Kanto Edition』 ISBN 978-4-634-50010-5</ref> <ref name=concept>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/info/prof.html Museum Purpose and Management Goals]</ref> <ref name=chibatopi>Chiba Nippo: "[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/local_kiji.php?i=nesp1290132141 1000-Year-Old Everyday Life Revealed Through Ink-Inscribed Pottery]" (November 19, 2010)</ref> <ref name=haniwa1>Nihon University Press: "[http://www.nu-press.net/archives/article000811.html Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Hosts Haniwa Festival, Displaying Around 20 Warrior Haniwa]" (July 23, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa2>Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Museum: "[http://www.chs.nihon-u.ac.jp/museum/exhibition.php?l=3&r=110131-163501 Haniwa Festival – Expressions in Clay]" (June 18 – July 28, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa3>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html Haniwa Festival]</ref> <ref name=haniwa4>Narita City Official Site: "[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/kanko/guide/sight-old-haniwa.html Narita City Tourism Guide]"</ref> <ref name=welcome>Ministry of Foreign Affairs: "[https://www.mlit.go.jp/kisha/kisha02/01/010704/010704_3_04.pdf Cultural and Tourism Promotion by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs]" (April 25, 2002)</ref> }} == See Also ==
* {{ill|Haniwa Day|ja|埴輪の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa Day|qq=}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of historical sites in the Kanto region|ja|関東地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of historical sites in the Kanto region|qq=}} * [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawadai Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaburaki Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsutsumi Gongen-zuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Bōsō Umayama Shell Mound|ja|房総姥山貝塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Umayama Shell Mound|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.haniwakan.com/index.html Official Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Website] * [http://www.chiba-web.com/chibahaku/24/index.html Chiba Prefecture Museum Association - Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum]

{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archaeological museum|qq=}} | Operated_by = [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]] | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibayama Town Board of Education|qq=}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]] 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], specializing in [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) and [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture Ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-Ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawadai Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaburaki Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|1=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]], specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|qq=}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==

{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archaeological museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博物館}} | Operated_by = [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]] | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibayama Town Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]] 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], specializing in [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) and [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture Ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-Ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawadai Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaburaki Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|1=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]], specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本大学文理学部・大学院文学研究科及び総合基礎科学研究科}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==

'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], specializing in [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) and [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta clay figures).

Chiba Prefecture Ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-Ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawadai Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaburaki Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|1=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]], specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本大学文理学部・大学院文学研究科及び総合基礎科学研究科}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==

* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本大学文理学部・大学院文学研究科及び総合基礎科学研究科}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>

revision.text:
{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archaeological museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博物館}} | Operated_by = [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]] | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibayama Town Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]] 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], specializing in [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) and [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture Ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-Ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawadai Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaburaki Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|1=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]], specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本大学文理学部・大学院文学研究科及び総合基礎科学研究科}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==
{{reflist|2|refs= <ref name=hyogo>{{ill|Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education|ja|兵庫県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education|qq=}}, "National Ranking of Archaeological Sites and Relic Quantities" ([http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/kihonkousou/6%284%29.pdf PDF])</ref> <ref name=zen>『Compilation of Keyhole-Shaped Kofun: Tohoku and Kanto Edition』 ISBN 978-4-634-50010-5</ref> <ref name=concept>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/info/prof.html Museum Purpose and Management Goals]</ref> <ref name=chibatopi>Chiba Nippo: "[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/local_kiji.php?i=nesp1290132141 1000-Year-Old Everyday Life Revealed Through Ink-Inscribed Pottery]" (November 19, 2010)</ref> <ref name=haniwa1>Nihon University Press: "[http://www.nu-press.net/archives/article000811.html Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Hosts Haniwa Festival, Displaying Around 20 Warrior Haniwa]" (July 23, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa2>Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Museum: "[http://www.chs.nihon-u.ac.jp/museum/exhibition.php?l=3&r=110131-163501 Haniwa Festival – Expressions in Clay]" (June 18 – July 28, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa3>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html Haniwa Festival]</ref> <ref name=haniwa4>Narita City Official Site: "[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/kanko/guide/sight-old-haniwa.html Narita City Tourism Guide]"</ref> <ref name=welcome>Ministry of Foreign Affairs: "[https://www.mlit.go.jp/kisha/kisha02/01/010704/010704_3_04.pdf Cultural and Tourism Promotion by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs]" (April 25, 2002)</ref> }} == See Also ==
* {{ill|Haniwa Day|ja|埴輪の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa Day|qq=}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of historical sites in the Kanto region|ja|関東地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of historical sites in the Kanto region|qq=}} * [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawadai Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaburaki Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsutsumi Gongen-zuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Bōsō Umayama Shell Mound|ja|房総姥山貝塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Umayama Shell Mound|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* [http://www.haniwakan.com/index.html Official Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Website] * [http://www.chiba-web.com/chibahaku/24/index.html Chiba Prefecture Museum Association - Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum]

revision.text:
{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archaeological museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博物館}} | Operated_by = [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]] | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibayama Town Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]] 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], specializing in [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) and [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture Ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-Ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawadai Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaburaki Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|1=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]], specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本大学文理学部・大学院文学研究科及び総合基礎科学研究科}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==
{{reflist|2|refs= <ref name=hyogo>{{ill|Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education|ja|兵庫県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education|qq=}}, "National Ranking of Archaeological Sites and Relic Quantities" ([http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/kihonkousou/6%284%29.pdf PDF])</ref> <ref name=zen>『Compilation of Keyhole-Shaped Kofun: Tohoku and Kanto Edition』 ISBN 978-4-634-50010-5</ref> <ref name=concept>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/info/prof.html Museum Purpose and Management Goals]</ref> <ref name=chibatopi>Chiba Nippo: "[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/local_kiji.php?i=nesp1290132141 1000-Year-Old Everyday Life Revealed Through Ink-Inscribed Pottery]" (November 19, 2010)</ref> <ref name=haniwa1>Nihon University Press: "[http://www.nu-press.net/archives/article000811.html Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Hosts Haniwa Festival, Displaying Around 20 Warrior Haniwa]" (July 23, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa2>Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Museum: "[http://www.chs.nihon-u.ac.jp/museum/exhibition.php?l=3&r=110131-163501 Haniwa Festival – Expressions in Clay]" (June 18 – July 28, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa3>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html Haniwa Festival]</ref> <ref name=haniwa4>Narita City Official Site: "[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/kanko/guide/sight-old-haniwa.html Narita City Tourism Guide]"</ref> <ref name=welcome>Ministry of Foreign Affairs: "[https://www.mlit.go.jp/kisha/kisha02/01/010704/010704_3_04.pdf Cultural and Tourism Promotion by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs]" (April 25, 2002)</ref> }} == See Also ==
* {{ill|Haniwa Day|ja|埴輪の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa Day|qq=}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of historical sites in the Kanto region|ja|関東地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of historical sites in the Kanto region|qq=}} * [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawadai Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaburaki Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsutsumi Gongen-zuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Bōsō Umayama Shell Mound|ja|房総姥山貝塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Umayama Shell Mound|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.haniwakan.com/index.html Official Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Website] * [http://www.chiba-web.com/chibahaku/24/index.html Chiba Prefecture Museum Association - Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum]

* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本大学文理学部・大学院文学研究科及び総合基礎科学研究科}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>

revision.text:
{{Infobox museum | Name = Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) | image = [[File:Shibayama Ancient-tomb and Clay-image Museum.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Museum building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E}}}} | Official_name = Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum<ref>[http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/reiki/reiki_honbun/g061RG00000155.html Shibayama Town Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum Establishment and Management Ordinance]</ref> | Nickname = haniwakan | Type = {{ill|Archaeological museum|ja|考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archaeological museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博物館}} | Operated_by = [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]] | Managed_by = {{ill|Shibayama Town Board of Education|ja|芝山町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibayama Town Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Established = 1988 (Shōwa 63) May 1 | Location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]] 438-1 | Coordinates = {{coord|35|41|39.0|N|140|25|42.0|E|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], specializing in [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) and [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture Ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-Ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawadai Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaburaki Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|1=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]], specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本大学文理学部・大学院文学研究科及び総合基礎科学研究科}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
== References ==
{{reflist|2|refs= <ref name=hyogo>{{ill|Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education|ja|兵庫県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education|qq=}}, "National Ranking of Archaeological Sites and Relic Quantities" ([http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/kihonkousou/6%284%29.pdf PDF])</ref> <ref name=zen>『Compilation of Keyhole-Shaped Kofun: Tohoku and Kanto Edition』 ISBN 978-4-634-50010-5</ref> <ref name=concept>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/info/prof.html Museum Purpose and Management Goals]</ref> <ref name=chibatopi>Chiba Nippo: "[http://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/chiba/local_kiji.php?i=nesp1290132141 1000-Year-Old Everyday Life Revealed Through Ink-Inscribed Pottery]" (November 19, 2010)</ref> <ref name=haniwa1>Nihon University Press: "[http://www.nu-press.net/archives/article000811.html Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Hosts Haniwa Festival, Displaying Around 20 Warrior Haniwa]" (July 23, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa2>Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences Museum: "[http://www.chs.nihon-u.ac.jp/museum/exhibition.php?l=3&r=110131-163501 Haniwa Festival – Expressions in Clay]" (June 18 – July 28, 2008)</ref> <ref name=haniwa3>Official Website: [http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html Haniwa Festival]</ref> <ref name=haniwa4>Narita City Official Site: "[http://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/kanko/guide/sight-old-haniwa.html Narita City Tourism Guide]"</ref> <ref name=welcome>Ministry of Foreign Affairs: "[https://www.mlit.go.jp/kisha/kisha02/01/010704/010704_3_04.pdf Cultural and Tourism Promotion by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs]" (April 25, 2002)</ref> }} == See Also ==
* {{ill|Haniwa Day|ja|埴輪の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa Day|qq=}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of historical sites in the Kanto region|ja|関東地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of historical sites in the Kanto region|qq=}} * [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawadai Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaburaki Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōtsutsumi Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|大堤権現塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsutsumi Gongen-zuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Bōsō Umayama Shell Mound|ja|房総姥山貝塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Umayama Shell Mound|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum''' (芝山古墳・はにわ博物館) is a municipal museum in [[:en:Shibayama, Chiba|Shibayama, Chiba]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], specializing in [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) and [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta clay figures).
== Overview ==
Chiba Prefecture Ranks second in Japan for the number of kofun, with '''13,112 Kofuns''', and third for the total volume of excavated relics.<ref name=hyogo/> Additionally, Chiba has the '''highest number of keyhole-shaped kofun''' (677 confirmed sites), surpassing the second-Ranking Ibaraki Prefecture (445 sites).<ref name=zen/> The Shibayama area is particularly rich in kofun dating from the '''5th to 8th centuries''', with a significant number of haniwa and other burial artifacts.
To preserve and present these historical assets in a '''clear and engaging way''', making them accessible to elementary school students and the general public, the museum was opened on '''May 1, 1988''' (Shōwa 63) within '''Shibayama Park'''.<ref name=concept/>
== Exhibitions ==
=== Exhibition Room 1 === * Fundamental exhibits explaining kofun and haniwa. * Haniwa artifacts excavated from kofun groups such as: ** {{ill|Ogawadai Kofun Group|ja|小川台古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawadai Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kaburaki Kofun Group|ja|蕪木古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaburaki Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|1=Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Hōma Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Hōma Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * Reproduction of '''funerary haniwa rows''' from the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]], specifically from '''Tonozuka Kofun''' and '''Himezuka Kofun'''.
=== Exhibition Room 2 === * '''Reconstructed ancient clothing''' based on haniwa findings. * '''Reconstruction of an ancient kofun-era dwelling'''.
=== Exhibition Room 3 === * '''Kofun distribution maps'''. * Exhibits showcasing the transition from '''the Kofun period to Buddhist adoption''', including Buddhist statues.
=== Other Exhibits === * The museum holds '''special exhibitions''' focusing on '''archaeological finds from the Shibayama region'''.<ref name=chibatopi/>
== Collaboration with Research Institutions ==
* The museum collaborates with '''{{ill|Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon University Faculty of Humanities and Sciences|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本大学文理学部・大学院文学研究科及び総合基礎科学研究科}}''', organizing exhibitions, lectures, and research programs.<ref name=haniwa1/><ref name=haniwa2/>
== Role as a Tourist Attraction ==
* The museum supports the '''annual "Shibayama Haniwa Festival" (芝山はにわ祭)''', held every '''November''', which recreates aspects of '''the Kofun period'''.<ref name=haniwa3/><ref name=haniwa4/> * It is a participating facility in the '''"Narita Welcome Card"''' initiative promoted by the '''Ministry of Foreign Affairs''', as part of the '''Narita International Tourism Model District'''.<ref name=welcome/>
